raw_comment: I noticed that the "First leaf" arches to the west coast of america except for the "First leaf" in May. The "First leaf" in May was a big clump in the middle of the west and little parts scattered on other places of the U.S.
ace_sentences: The "First leaf" arches to the west coast of america.
The "First leaf" in May is an exception.
The "First leaf" in May was a big clump in the middle of the west.
The "First leaf" in May was little parts scattered on other places of the U.S.

The \"First leaf\" arches to the west co: the "First leaf" arches to the west coast of america
The \"First leaf\" in May is an exceptio: except for the "First leaf" in May
The \"First leaf\" in May was a big clum: The "First leaf" in May was a big clump in the middle of the west
The \"First leaf\" in May was little par: little parts scattered on other places of the U.S.
